name: Furi MCP Manager
what_it_does: Furi MCP manager enabling developers to manage multiple MCP servers — starting, stopping, and monitoring MCP server processes, managing MCP server configurations, providing a unified CLI interface for MCP server lifecycle management, and simplifying the orchestration of multiple MCP servers in development environments.
use_cases: Starting and stopping multiple MCP servers from a single CLI from developers
Managing MCP server configurations and environment variables
Monitoring running MCP server processes from development tooling
Simplifying MCP development workflow with process management
Organizing and running multiple MCP servers for complex agent setups
Streamlining local MCP server orchestration during development
not_for: Production MCP deployment (designed for development environments)
End users of MCP servers (developer tooling for MCP builders)
Containerized deployments (use Docker/Kubernetes for production orchestration)
best_when: A developer managing multiple local MCP servers needs a simple CLI to start/stop and configure them — Furi provides process management without Docker overhead.
avoid_when: You need production-grade MCP deployment or container orchestration.

auth.notes: No authentication — local process manager. MCP server credentials managed per-server in configuration files.

pricing.notes: Free open source MCP process manager.

known_agent_gotchas: Process manager tool, not an MCP server — used to manage other MCP servers
Community tool from individual developer — limited documentation
Early-stage project — API and features may change
Process management may conflict with manual server starts
Configuration file format may evolve between versions
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local process manager. No credentials for Furi itself. Managed server credentials stored in config files — protect config files. Community tool.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local process manager. Config files may contain MCP server credentials — protect appropriately. Community tool.
